creator: marino@FreeBSD.org
text:
It is standard procedure for -devel ports to set IGNORE when they are older than stable ports.

I am the wrong person to ask about this because I am very much against -devel ports in general.   Many times there existence in not justified in my opinion.

For example, if 1.9 comes out, why is not the regular openmpi updated instead?  There are only a couple of ports dependent on it, so I don't see why the upgrade can't be coordinated.


My vote would be remove it and never bring it back -- but you're the current maintainer so it would a) be your decision and b) your action (unless you release maintainership for the sole purpose of making somebody else remove it which has been done before but it's not very cool.) 

:)
